overview: A single Go-based RESTful backend service exposes versioned `/v1` endpoints for managing todo items, persisting data in a relational database via versioned migrations. The service handles validation, structured errors, health/readiness checks, graceful shutdown, and pagination/filtering/sorting on list endpoints.
requirements: CRUD operations for todo items with title, description, due date, priority, completion status
List endpoint supports pagination (limit/offset), filter by status/priority/due-date range, sort by created/due/priority asc/desc
Validation: title 1-200 chars, description ≤2000 chars, priority in {low,medium,high}, ISO8601 due dates
Structured JSON errors with code, message, optional field details
Versioned URL prefix `/v1`; unknown routes return 404 in error schema; malformed JSON returns 400
Durable persistence in relational DB with versioned migrations applied automatically
Safe concurrent updates (optimistic concurrency or row locking)
Configurable via env vars (DB connection, port); separate dev/stage/prod configs; no hard-coded secrets
/health endpoint and /ready endpoint that verifies DB connectivity
Request logging (method, path, status, latency); error logging with context
Max request body size enforced; input sanitized; graceful shutdown of in-flight requests
DB outage returns 503 instead of crashing
List endpoint <200ms for 100k items; supports 100 concurrent requests; indexed queries
OpenAPI spec and automated tests covering CRUD, validation, and error paths

components.name: todo-api
components.componentAgentInstructions:
Build a Go (1.22+) backend using net/http on port 9090. Single binary `todo-api`.

Routing: implement a small router (or use http.ServeMux with method dispatch). All resource endpoints under `/v1` prefix. Endpoints: GET /health (always 200 JSON {status:ok}), GET /v1/ready (pings DB; 200 or 503), GET/POST /v1/todos, GET/PUT/DELETE /v1/todos/{id}.

Persistence: PostgreSQL via `database/sql` + `github.com/lib/pq` (or pgx). Use SQLite as fallback if env DB_DRIVER=sqlite for local. Configure via env: DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD, DB_NAME, DB_SSLMODE, PORT (default 9090), MAX_BODY_BYTES (default 1MB), ENV (dev/staging/prod).

Migrations: embed SQL files via embed.FS and run on startup. Schema: todos(id UUID PK, title TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(length<=200), description TEXT, completed BOOL NOT NULL DEFAULT false, due_date TIMESTAMPTZ NULL, priority TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'medium' CHECK in (low,medium,high), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), version INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1). Indexes on (completed), (priority), (due_date), (created_at).

IDs: generate UUIDv4 (`github.com/google/uuid`).

Validation: title required, 1-200 chars after trim; description ≤2000; priority must be in enum; due_date must parse as RFC3339. Return 400 with ErrorResponse {code:"validation_error", message, details:[{field,message}]}.

List: parse limit (default 20, max 100), offset (default 0), filters completed/priority/due_from/due_to, sort in {created_at,due_date,priority}, order asc/desc. Build parameterized SQL. Return {items,total,limit,offset}.

Update: use optimistic concurrency — increment version, WHERE id=$ AND version=$old; if 0 rows affected and id exists → 409 conflict; if not found → 404.

Delete: 204 on success, 404 with error body if missing.

Middleware: request logging (method, path, status, latency in ms), recover panics → 500, max body limit via http.MaxBytesReader, content-type enforcement on POST/PUT, malformed JSON → 400 {code:"invalid_json"}.

Unknown routes → 404 {code:"not_found"}.

DB outage handling: wrap queries; on connection errors return 503 {code:"unavailable"}.

Graceful shutdown: trap SIGINT/SIGTERM, http.Server.Shutdown with 30s timeout.

Logging: use slog with JSON handler; never log secrets or full request bodies.

Dockerfile: multi-stage build, distroless or alpine base, expose 9090.

Provide unit tests for handlers and validation, plus integration tests using sqlite in-memory.
